name: QA 681
description:
PCP QA Test No. 681

Exercise __pmProcessExec() and friends from libpcp with valgrind

... same test as 036 (that one's without valgrind)



Copyright (c) 2017-2018 Ken McDonell.  All Rights Reserved.



**To reproduce this test in a container, please run:**

    ./build/ci/run.py --runner container --platform debian11 reproduce

output mismatch (see 681.out.bad)
70,76c70
< my arg echoer ...
< [0] TMP.sh
< [1] blah;trickme|foo
< [2] cat
< [3] and
< [4] dog
< __pmProcessExec -> 0
---
> __pmProcessExec -> 101 exit=101
79c73
< __pmProcessExec: pid=PID wait_pid=PID exit=0
---
> __pmProcessExec: pid=PID wait_pid=PID exit=101
82a77,79
> execve(0x50be220(TMP.sh), 0x50be4b0, 0x1fff000288) failed, errno 13
> EXEC FAILED: I can't recover from execve() failing, so I'm dying.
> Add more stringent tests in PRE(sys_execve), or work out how to recover.
